questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows two coherent microwave source $S_1$ and $S_2$ emitting waves of wavelength $\lambda$ and separated by a distance $3 \lambda$. For $\lambda \ll \mathrm{D}$ and $y \neq 0$, the minimum value of $y$ for point $P$ to be an intensity maximum is $\frac{\sqrt{m} \mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{n}}$. Determine the value of $\mathrm{m}+\mathrm{n}$, if m and n are coprime numbers.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a YDSE two thin transparent sheets are used in front of the slits $S_1$ and $S_2 \mu_1=1.6$ and $\mu_2=1.4$. If both sheets have thickness ' $t$ ', the central maximum is observed at a distance of 5 mm from centre $O$. Now the sheets are replaced by two sheets of same material of refractive index $\frac{\mu_1+\mu_2}{2}$ but having thickness $t_1$ and $t_2$ such that ' $t$ ' = $\frac{t_1+t_2}{2}$. Now central maximum is observed at distance of 8 mm from centre $O$ on the same side as before. Find the thickness $t_1$ (in $\mu \mathrm{m}$ ). [Given $d=1 \mathrm{~mm}, D=1 \mathrm{~m}$ ] (mark the answer in $\mu \mathrm{~m}$)
questions.solution:
$\Delta \mathrm{x}=\left\{\left(\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{P}-\mathrm{t}_2\right)+\mu_2 \mathrm{t}_2\right\}-\left\{\left(\mathrm{S}_1 \mathrm{P}-\mathrm{t}_1\right)+\mu_1 \mathrm{t}_1\right\}=\left(\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{P}-\mathrm{S}_1 \mathrm{P}\right)+\left\{\left(\mu_2-1\right) \mathrm{t}_2-\left(\mu_1-1\right) \mathrm{t}_1\right\}$
(i) In first case :
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{y}_1=\frac{\mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{~d}}\left\{\mathrm{t}\left(\mu_1-1\right)-\mathrm{t}\left(\mu_2-1\right)\right\}=\frac{\mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{~d}}\left\{\mathrm{t}\left(\mu_1-\mu_2\right)\right\} \\
& \text { or } \mathrm{t}=\frac{5 \times 10^{-3} \times 1 \times 10^{-3}}{1 \times(1.6-1.4)} \\
& \therefore \quad \mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2=5 \times 10^{-5}
\end{aligned}$......(A)
(ii) When both sheets have same refractive index
$\begin{aligned}
& \mu=\frac{1.6+1.4}{2}=1.5 \\
& \mathrm{y}_2=\frac{\mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{~d}}\left\{\mathrm{t}_1(\mu-1)-\mathrm{t}_2(\mu-1)\right\}=\frac{\mathrm{D}}{\mathrm{~d}}\left\{(\mu-1)\left(\mathrm{t}_1-\mathrm{t}_2\right)\right\} \\
& \therefore \mathrm{t}_1-\mathrm{t}_2=\frac{8 \times 10^{-3} \times 1 \times 10^{-3}}{1 \times(1.5-1)}=\frac{8 \times 10^{-6}}{0.5}=1.6 \times 10^{-5}
\end{aligned}$......(B)

On solving, $\mathrm{t}_1=33 \mu \mathrm{~m}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a YDSE experiment the two slits are covered with a transparent membrane of negligible thickness which allows light to pass through it but does not allow water. A glass slab of thickness $\mathrm{t}=0.41 \mathrm{~mm}$ and refractive index $\mu_3=1.5$ is placed infront of one of the slits as shown in the figure. The separation between the slits is $\mathrm{d}=0.30 \mathrm{~mm}$. The entire space to the left of the slits is filled with water of refractive index $\mu_w=4 / 3$. A coherent light of intensity I and absolute wavelength $\lambda=5000 \mathrm{~A}^{\circ}$ is being incident on the slits making an angle $30^{\circ}$ with horizontal. If screen is placed at a distance $\mathrm{D}=1 \mathrm{~m}$ from the slits, find (a) the position of central maxima. (mark in answer in cm)
questions.solution: (a) Let central maxima lies at point P at a distance x from the central line. Then optical path difference $\Delta p$ at point $P$ is $\begin{aligned} & \left(\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{P}-\mathrm{t}\right)+\mathrm{t} \mu_{\mathrm{B}}-\mu_{\mathrm{w}} \mathrm{~d} \sin \theta-\mathrm{S}_1 \mathrm{P} \\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{~S}_2 \mathrm{P}-\mathrm{S}_1 \mathrm{P}+\left(\mu_B-1\right) \mathrm{t}-\mu_{\mathrm{w}} \mathrm{~d} \sin \theta \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{\mathrm{xd}}{\mathrm{D}}+\left(\mu_{\mathrm{B}}-1\right) \mathrm{t}-\mu_{\mathrm{v}} \mathrm{~d} \sin \theta \end{aligned}$ For central maxima $\Delta \mathrm{p}=0$ $\begin{aligned} x & =\frac{D}{d}\left[\left(\mu_w d \sin \theta\right)-\left(\mu_g-1\right) t\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{3 \times 10^{-4}}\left[\frac{4}{3} \times 3 \times 10^{-4} \times \frac{1}{2}-0.5 \times 0.41 \times 10^{-3}\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{3 \times 10^{-4}}\left[2 \times 10^{-4}-2.05 \times 10^{-4}\right]=-\frac{5 \times 10^{-6}}{3 \times 10^{-4}} \\ & =-1.66 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}=-1.66 \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned}$ Central maxima lies at a distance 1.66 cm below the central line
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the Young's double slit experiment shown in the figure, the medium between slit plane and screen has refraction index varying with times as $\mathrm{n}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{0}}+\mathrm{kt}$ (a) find the y co-ordinates of central maxima
questions.solution: (a) $\mathrm{S}_1 \mathrm{P}-\mathrm{S}_2 \mathrm{P}=\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{D}}$ $\Delta x=\left(\mathbf{n}_0+k t\right) \frac{d y}{D}-d \sin \phi=0$ For central maxima. $\therefore y=\frac{D \sin \phi}{n_0+k t} y$-coordinates of central maximum
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a young double slit experiment. The two slits are illuminated by a monochromatic light source $S$ of wavelength $\lambda=500 \mathrm{~nm}$. Distance between slits and screen is $D=2 \mathrm{~m}$ and distance between two slits $S_1 S_2=10 \mu \mathrm{m}$. $\ell_1=20 \mu \mathrm{m}$ and $\ell_2=\mu \mathrm{m}$, as shown in the figure. If maxima will appear on the screen is 20 K . Find the value of K .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a young's double slit experiment one of the slits is covered by a thin film of thickness $\mathrm{t}=0.04 \mathrm{~mm}$, and refractive index $\mu=1.2+$ $\frac{9 \times 10^{-14} \mathrm{~m}^2}{\lambda^2}$, where, $\lambda$ is the wave length in meter. A beam of light consisting two wavelengths $\lambda_1=400 \mathrm{~nm}$ and $\lambda_2=600 \mathrm{~nm}$ falls normally on the plane of the slits. Find the distance between two central maxima in milimeter. Distance of screen from slits is 400 times the separation between the slits.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The intensity received at the focus of the lens is I when no glass slab has been placed in front of the slit. Both the slits are of the same dimension and the plane wavefront incident perpendicularly on them, has wavelength $\lambda$. On placing the glass slab, the intensity reduces to $3 \mathrm{I} / 4$ at the focus. Find out the minimum thickness of the glass slab (in $Å$ ) if its refractive index is $3 / 2$. Given $\lambda=6933 Å$, $\mu=1.5$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
